g.text: Ouroboros SPSC Ring Buffer — Lock-Free Operation
2. If enough space: memcpy data at tail position
Release fence ensures data is visible before tail moves
2. If data available: read from head position
Release fence ensures read is complete before head moves
Only atomic load/store on head & tail.
No modulo needed if capacity is 2^N.
Reader can get pointer to data
spsc::RingBuffer 1 writer, 1 reader Lock-free (atomics only) Used by aether IPC (IpcRing)
mpsc::RingBuffer N writers, 1 reader Lock-free (CAS on tail) For multi-producer logging / event queues
spmc::RingBuffer 1 writer, N readers Lock-free (CAS on head) For fan-out / broadcast patterns
In Aether IPC: two spsc::RingBuffer instances are placement-new'd into shared memory (512KB total).
Ring 0 (256KB): client → server (requests). Ring 1 (256KB): server → client (responses + notifications).
ServiceBase uses sendMutex to serialize txRing writes — making the server side act as a single producer despite
multiple code paths (receiverLoop responses + sendNotify broadcasts) writing to the same ring.
